title: Magnetic ground state of a prototype quasicrystal approximant: a candidate for octahedral spin ice physics
authors: Leonie Woodland, Farid Labib, Dmitry Khalyavin, Pascal Manuel, Fabio Orlandi, Hubertus Luetkens, Ryuji Tamura
abstract: Magnetic ordering in quasicrystals has recently emerged as a fertile ground for discovering unconventional magnetic states beyond the framework of periodic crystals. However, elucidating the microscopic origin of such states remains challenging due to the intrinsic aperiodicity of quasicrystals. Here, we address this issue by investigating the prototypical Tsai-type quasicrystal approximant Cd6Tb, which preserves the essential local geometry and connectivity of icosahedral quasicrystals while allowing detailed structural and magnetic characterization due to its translational periodicity. Using neutron diffraction measurements, we find a noncoplanar multi-k magnetic ground state composed of Ising-like Tb moments arranged on a network of corner-sharing octahedra, the ingredients required to host octahedral spin-ice physics. Remarkably, only one third of the Tb moments develop long-range magnetic order, whereas the remaining moments display strongly reduced static order accompanied by persistent spin dynamics on microsecond timescales, as evidenced by muon spin rotation. This coexistence of ordered and fluctuating moments constitutes a potential realization of magnetic fragmentation - a key prediction of octahedral spin-ice physics - in a quasicrystal-related material.
summary: This paper identifies a candidate for octahedral spin-ice physics in the quasicrystal approximant Cd6Tb. By combining neutron diffraction and muon spin rotation, the authors demonstrate that the magnetic ground state consists of both ordered and fluctuating spin components, a phenomenon known as magnetic fragmentation.
main_problem: The study aims to resolve the microscopic magnetic ground state of the Tsai-type quasicrystal approximant Cd6Tb and determine if it realizes magnetic fragmentation, a key prediction of octahedral spin-ice physics.
main_result: The researchers discovered a noncoplanar multi-k magnetic ground state where one-third of the Tb moments are ordered and two-thirds exhibit persistent spin dynamics, providing a potential realization of magnetic fragmentation.
method: Neutron powder diffraction was used for magnetic structure refinement, while Muon Spin Rotation (muSR) and AC susceptibility were employed to investigate spin dynamics and distinguish between static and dynamic magnetism.
model_or_system: The system is Cd6Tb, a 1/1-cubic quasicrystal approximant featuring a network of corner-sharing octahedra with Ising-like Tb3+ moments.
key_observables: Neutron diffraction patterns, magnetic propagation vectors (k1 and k2), magnetic moments (apical vs. equatorial), muSR asymmetry/relaxation rates, and AC susceptibility.
important_parameters: Magnetic ordering temperature (~20 K), persistent dynamics on microsecond timescales, and the reduction of equatorial moments to ~4.62 uB compared to apical moments of ~10.2 uB.
main_assumptions: The structural refinement used a cubic model because the subtle monoclinic distortion could not be fully resolved in powder neutron diffraction; diffraction alone could not distinguish between certain relative phases of the magnetic structure.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows neutron diffraction data and structural fits; Fig 2 illustrates the refined magnetic structures for apical and equatorial spins and the spin-flip model; Fig 3 shows the frustrated Heisenberg exchange model.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of quasicrystal magnetism, describes the structural and magnetic characterization of Cd6Tb via neutron diffraction, presents the discovery of the multi-k ground state, uses muSR to prove the presence of persistent dynamics, and concludes with the identification of magnetic fragmentation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Powder neutron diffraction data on Cd6Tb. (a) Data at 30 K, 1.5 K and the fit to the 30 K data. Inset: The po- sitions of Tb3+ ions in Cd6Tb, with the icosahedral arrange- ment highlighted. (b) Magnetic diffraction pattern obtained by taking the difference between the 1.5 K and 30 K data in (a), together with the magnetic refinement (solid black line). The difference curve (blue) has been offset by 4 units for clar- ity.

FIG. 2. Magnetic structure of Cd6Tb. (a,b) The refined mag- netic structures of the apical (z = 0, k2) spins (a) and equa- torial (k1) spins (b) in Cd6Tb. The black outlines in both panels indicate a 2 × 2 × 1 magnetic supercell. In (a), down spins are highlighted in yellow for clarity. (c) The measured (average) moment can be half of the full moment if one ran- domly chosen spin in every four is flipped.

FIG. 3. A two-dimensional version of the proposed model of frustrated Heisenberg exchange interactions. Gray dashed lines indicate the projection of the octahedra. Solid circles correspond to one of the three separate sets of spins sharing a common easy axis. J′ 1 is only different from J1 for the apical spins.

FIG. 4. µSR data taken at 5 K. The main panel shows the long time decay of the asymmetry while the inset shows a zoomed in view of the short time oscillatory behaviour. Error bars correspond to one standard deviation statistical uncertainty.
